Quercus suber, commonly known as the Cork oak, is a tree species native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its thick, bark that is harvested for cork production. The health benefits of Quercus suber include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Quercus suber include polyphenolic compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, as well as terpenoids and triterpenoids. The most common herbal preparations made with Quercus suber are infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to support digestive health and reduce oxidative stress.
